description: Momentum strategy based on factor returns rather than individual stock returns, where factors that have performed well recently continue to outperform
methodology: Rank factors based on recent performance and go long top-performing factors while shorting poor-performing factors

description: Traditional momentum strategy based on individual stock price momentum, where stocks with strong recent performance continue to outperform
methodology: Rank stocks based on recent return performance and construct long-short portfolios

description: The sensitivity or loading of individual stocks to specific risk factors
methodology: Calculate factor betas through regression of stock returns on factor returns

description: Momentum effect observed in individual stock returns across different time lags
methodology: Analysis of momentum patterns in stock returns at various lag periods

description: Momentum effect observed in factor returns across different time lags
methodology: Analysis of momentum patterns in factor returns at various lag periods

description: A phenomenon where factors that have performed well in the recent past tend to continue performing well in the future
methodology: Sort factors based on past performance and analyze persistence of returns across time periods

description: The tendency for stocks that have performed well (poorly) in the recent past to continue performing well (poorly) in the near future
methodology: Sort stocks based on past returns and analyze continuation of performance patterns

description: Momentum effect observed in factor returns, where factors that have performed well continue to perform well
methodology: Analysis of persistent returns in factors over time

description: Momentum effect observed in individual stock returns, where stocks that have performed well continue to perform well
methodology: Analysis of persistent returns in individual stocks over time

description: Collection of 72 academic signals used to construct factors from published literature
data_required: Signal construction variables as per literature
methodology: Replication of factor construction as long-short dollar-neutral portfolios with dynamic market exposure hedging

description: Portfolio constructed by taking equal-risk weighted average of multiple factors
methodology: Equal-risk weighting of 72 factors to create diversified factor portfolio

description: A strategy that buys the most successful factors and sells the least performing ones based on relative performance rankings
methodology: Rank factors by performance and construct long-short portfolio based on relative performance

description: A strategy that buys factors with past positive returns and sells factors with past negative returns
methodology: Construct portfolio by buying factors with positive past returns and selling factors with negative past returns

description: An equally-weighted portfolio of factors based on risk contribution rather than dollar allocation
methodology: Weight factors equally by risk contribution to create balanced risk exposure

description: Portfolio construction methodology that maintains zero net dollar exposure
methodology: Construct portfolio with equal dollar amounts in long and short positions
